div.p: The HL7 v2+ specification defines the standard for electronic health data exchange between healthcare information systems. It is the most widely implemented healthcare interoperability standard in the world, used by over 95% of US healthcare organizations and extensively deployed internationally.

HL7 v2+ is the evolution of the HL7 Version 2.x messaging standard. It consolidates and updates the normative content from previous versions (2.1 through 2.9.1) into a single, continuously maintained specification. The "+" designation indicates that this specification is a living document that will be updated incrementally rather than through major version releases.

HL7 v2+ messages are composed of segments, which are logical groupings of related data fields. Segments are arranged according to message structures (abstract syntax trees) that define the valid composition and ordering of segments for each message type. Messages are triggered by healthcare events (such as a patient admission, an order being placed, or a result being reported).

Data within segments is typed using HL7 v2+ data types, which range from simple primitives (strings, numbers, dates) to complex composite types (extended person names, coded elements with exceptions, extended addresses).
The main sections of this IG are:

ul.li: Domains — Functional groupings of related messaging capabilities (Administration, Clinical, Financial, Technical)
Message Structures — Abstract syntax trees defining the arrangement of segments within messages
Segment Definitions — The 192 segments that serve as building blocks for messages
Data Types — Primitive and complex data types used in segment fields
Data Type Flavors — Constrained variants of data types for specific use cases
Terminology — Code systems and value sets referenced by the specification
Control — Message processing rules, acknowledgment patterns, and infrastructure
Conformance — Rules for specifying implementation profiles and compliance
